title: Insider Buying Highlights 3 Undervalued Small Caps In Asian Markets
content:
As Asian markets navigate a landscape marked by geopolitical developments and economic data releases, small-cap stocks are capturing attention with their potential for growth amid broader market movements. In this context, insider buying can serve as a valuable indicator of confidence in these companies' prospects, offering insights into which stocks might be positioned to capitalize on current conditions.

Top 10 Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Buying In Asia

Name PE PS Discount to Fair Value Value Rating Security Bank 4.2x 0.9x 34.35% ★★★★★★ Dicker Data 19.5x 0.6x -6.06% ★★★★☆☆ Shoucheng Holdings 41.7x 9.0x 44.41% ★★★☆☆☆ Centurion 12.1x 4.1x -22.26% ★★★☆☆☆ East West Banking 3.4x 0.9x 9.96% ★★★☆☆☆ DUG Technology 124.1x 2.7x 31.33% ★★★☆☆☆ SiteMinder NA 3.5x 45.02% ★★★☆☆☆ CapitaLand China Trust NA 4.0x -5.50% ★★★☆☆☆ Strike Energy NA 5.4x 34.86% ★★★☆☆☆ Nufarm NA 0.3x -118.87% ★★★☆☆☆

Click here to see the full list of 55 stocks from our Undervalued Asian Small Caps With Insider Buying screener.

Let's explore several standout options from the results in the screener.

Iress

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Iress is a technology company that provides software solutions for the financial services industry, with operations spanning wealth management, trading, and global market data sectors, and has a market capitalization of approximately A$1.77 billion.

Operations: Revenue is primarily generated from Trading & Global Market Data, Apac Wealth Management, and UK Wealth & Sourcing segments. The company has seen fluctuations in its gross profit margin, with a notable decline to 24.83% in mid-2023 before recovering to 36.56% by the end of 2025. Operating expenses have been substantial, particularly in General & Administrative costs, impacting overall profitability.

PE: 16.6x

Iress, a smaller player in the financial technology sector, has shown insider confidence with share purchases over the past year. Despite a drop in 2025 sales to A$556 million from A$601 million, they forecast revenue growth of 3-5% for 2026. The company faces higher risk due to reliance on external borrowing but remains committed to enhancing service excellence and customer satisfaction through strategic leadership changes. With earnings projected to grow annually by over 6%, Iress aims for steady progress.

Navigate through the intricacies of Iress with our comprehensive valuation report here. Assess Iress' past performance with our detailed historical performance reports.ASX:IRE Share price vs Value as at Apr 2026

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Hallenstein Glasson Holdings operates as a retail company specializing in fashion apparel through its Hallensteins and Glassons brands in New Zealand and Australia, with a market cap of NZ$1.14 billion.

Story Continues

Operations: Hallensteins, Glassons Australia, and Glassons New Zealand generate revenue of NZ$111.57 million, NZ$281.06 million, and NZ$129.28 million respectively. The company experienced a notable increase in gross profit margin from 56.65% in August 2016 to 60.57% in April 2026. Operating expenses primarily consist of sales and marketing costs, which reached up to NZ$189.50 million by early 2026.

PE: 12.8x

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings, a key player in the retail sector, shows promising growth potential despite relying solely on external borrowing. Their recent earnings report for the half year ending February 01, 2026, revealed sales of NZ$275.2 million and net income of NZ$28.01 million, marking an increase from last year. Insider confidence is evident with active share purchases over recent months. Looking ahead, projected annual earnings growth at 7% suggests potential value for investors seeking opportunities in smaller companies within Asia's dynamic market landscape.

Click here to discover the nuances of Hallenstein Glasson Holdings with our detailed analytical valuation report. Review our historical performance report to gain insights into Hallenstein Glasson Holdings''s past performance.NZSE:HLG Share price vs Value as at Apr 2026

Century Pacific Food

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Century Pacific Food is a diversified food company engaged in the production and distribution of meat, marine, milk, and emerging products with a market capitalization of ₱127.45 billion.

Operations: Century Pacific Food generates revenue primarily from its Meat, Marine, and Milk and Emerging segments, with the latter being the largest contributor at ₱37.75 billion. The company's gross profit margin has shown variation over time, reaching 25.35% by the end of 2025. Operating expenses are a significant cost factor, including general and administrative expenses which have been notable in size.

PE: 15.8x

Century Pacific Food, a smaller company in Asia's food sector, shows promising growth with sales rising to PHP 83.3 billion for 2025 from PHP 75.5 billion the previous year. Net income also increased to PHP 7.1 billion, reflecting solid performance despite higher risk funding through external borrowing. Recent executive changes saw Rosanne Sampani stepping up as Chief Audit Executive, indicating stable leadership transition. Forecasted earnings growth of nearly 10% annually suggests potential for future expansion and value realization in the market.

title: Discover 3 Promising Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Action In The Asian Market
content:
Amidst the backdrop of heightened geopolitical tensions and fluctuating energy prices, Asian markets have been navigating a complex landscape marked by cautious central bank policies and persistent inflation concerns. Despite these challenges, small-cap stocks in Asia present intriguing opportunities for investors seeking growth potential, especially when insider activity suggests confidence in their future prospects.

Top 10 Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Buying In Asia

Here's a peek at a few of the choices from the screener.

Ainsworth Game Technology

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Ainsworth Game Technology is a company that designs, manufactures, and supplies gaming machines and related equipment and services, with a market capitalization of A$186.94 million.

Operations: Ainsworth Game Technology generates revenue primarily from the sale of gaming machines and related services, with recent figures showing A$290.78 million in revenue. The company's gross profit margin has experienced fluctuations, reaching 64.23% at its peak but more recently recorded at 57.30%. Operating expenses are a significant cost component, consistently exceeding A$100 million in recent periods.

PE: -19.9x

Ainsworth Game Technology, a small company in the gaming industry, has recently seen insider confidence with Kjerulf Ainsworth proposing to increase their stake by 5.08% for A$22.24 million as of March 2026. Despite reporting a net loss of A$19.25 million for 2025 and facing goodwill impairment of A$43.1 million, the company's inclusion in the S&P/ASX Emerging Companies Index suggests potential growth prospects, with earnings forecasted to grow annually by 61.5%.

Iress

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Iress is a technology company providing software solutions for financial services, with operations spanning wealth management, trading, and global market data, holding a market cap of A$1.55 billion.

Story Continues

Operations: Revenue is primarily generated from Trading & Global Market Data, Apac Wealth Management, and UK Wealth & Sourcing. The gross profit margin has shown variability, reaching 36.56% in recent periods. Operating expenses include notable allocations to D&A and General & Administrative expenses.

PE: 16.8x

Iress, a company with a focus on wealth management software, recently projected 2026 revenue between A$520 million and A$528 million, showing potential growth. Despite a dip in 2025 earnings to A$79.26 million from the previous year, insider confidence is evident with recent share purchases by key figures. The appointment of Dominic Rey aims to enhance platform stability and efficiency in South Africa's wealth management sector. With strategic leadership changes and improved client engagement, Iress positions itself for future expansion.

Region Group

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Region Group operates in the real estate sector, focusing on convenience-based retail properties, with a market capitalization of A$3.19 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue primarily from convenience-based retail properties, with recent figures showing A$386.7 million in revenue. Over the years, the gross profit margin has shown fluctuations, reaching 64.73% in late 2025 and early 2026. Operating expenses have varied but were recorded at A$27.5 million during the same period.

PE: 8.1x

Region Group, a small player in Asia, has demonstrated insider confidence through significant share repurchases. Between July 2025 and March 2026, they bought back 11.9 million shares for A$28.1 million. Despite earnings forecasted to decline by 1.6% annually over the next three years, their recent half-year results showed net income soaring to A$180 million from A$81.8 million year-on-year, hinting at potential resilience amidst financial challenges and strategic buybacks extending into April 2027.

title: UPDATE -- Tidal Financial Group and Defiance ETFs Announce Reverse Stock Splits for Select ETFs
content:
NEW YORK, March  12, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Tidal Financial Group and Defiance ETFs announce reverse stock splits for twenty four of Defiance’s exchange-traded funds (“ETFs”). These reverse splits are intended to reduce the number of outstanding shares and proportionally increase each fund’s share price, with no impact on the total value of shareholders’ investments.

The reverse stock splits will apply to shareholders of record as of the date found in the table below.

ETF1:366.67%88636J28788636Y854NYSE03/19/26VSTLDefiance Daily Target 2X Long VST ETF1:366.67%88636R18088636Y300Nasdaq03/19/26HOOXDefiance Daily Target 2X Long HOOD ETF1:250%88636X71688636Y706CBOE03/20/26RKTLDefiance Daily Target 2X Long RKT ETF1:250%88636X64188636Y805CBOE03/20/26QSUDefiance Daily Target 2X Long QS ETF1:683.33%88636W63588636Y862NYSE03/20/26IREDefiance Daily Target 2X Long IREN ETF1:475%88636W65088636Y888NYSE03/20/26MPLDefiance Daily Target 2X Long MP ETF1:366.67%88636W64388636Y870NYSE03/20/26DKNXDefiance Daily Target 2X Long DKNG ETF1:580%88636R48788636W213Nasdaq03/20/26ORCXDefiance Daily Target 2X Long ORCL ETF1:366.67%88636R51188636W197Nasdaq03/20/26MST*Defiance Leveraged Long + Income MSTR ETF1:785.71%88636W27088636W189Nasdaq03/23/26RKLZDefiance Daily Target 2X Short RKLB ETF1:887.50%88636V82788636W171Nasdaq03/23/26OSCXDefiance Daily Target 2X Long OSCR ETF1:475%88636W73488636W122Nasdaq03/23/26STSMDefiance Daily Target 2X Short TSM ETF1:366.67%88636W43788636W163NYSE03/23/26OKLSDefiance Daily Target 2X Short OKLO ETF1:366.67%88636W46088636W155NYSE03/23/26AVXXDefiance Daily Target 2X Long AVAV ETF1:366.67%88636W79188636W114Nasdaq03/23/26QPUXDefiance 2X Daily Long Pure Quantum ETF1:366.67%88636V40588636W130Nasdaq03/23/26QBTZDefiance Daily Target 2X Short QBTS ETF1:366.67%88636V64588636W148Nasdaq03/23/26

*Due to DTC processing limitations, two corporate actions cannot occur in the same week. As a result, MST will not declare a distribution for the week of March 16 (Declaration date: 3/17; Ex-Date and Record Date: 3/18; Payable Date: 3/19), and the distribution rate for that week will be $0.00.

The regular weekly distribution schedule will resume the following week of March 23 (Declaration date: 3/24; Ex-Date and Record Date: 3/25; Payable Date: 3/26), and that distribution will include the amount that would have been distributed the prior week.

After the reverse stock splits, shareholders will receive one share for the number of shares indicated in the applicable ratio. For example, a 1-for-4 split means every four pre-split shares will be combined into one post-split share.

The total market value of each investor’s holdings will remain unchanged immediately following the reverse split, as the share price will adjust proportionally to reflect the reduced number of shares.

No action is required by current shareholders. The Depository Trust Company (“DTC”) will handle the splits automatically, and updated share balances will appear in shareholder accounts following the effective dates.

Fractional Shares and Tax Implications

The reverse split may result in some shareholders being entitled to fractional shares. Shareholders with fractional shares may receive cash compensation equivalent to the value of the fraction, subject to the policies of their broker. This redemption may have tax implications, and shareholders are advised to consult their tax advisors for personalized guidance. There will be no transaction fee for redeeming fractional shares.

About Defiance ETFs

Defiance ETFs is a leading issuer of thematic and leveraged exchange-traded funds designed to provide investors with exposure to disruptive innovation and emerging technologies. Defiance empowers investors with tactical tools to express forward-looking views on markets and sectors at the forefront of change.

For more information, please visit www.defianceetfs.com.

About Tidal Financial Group

Tidal Financial Group provides a comprehensive platform for ETF innovation, helping asset managers and investment firms bring differentiated strategies to market. Tidal delivers end-to-end ETF solutions, including product design, operations, compliance, marketing, and distribution — enabling partners like Defiance to focus on strategy and performance.

Learn more at www.tidalfinancialgroup.com.

Important Information:

Leveraged ETFs are not suitable for all investors and may be more volatile than traditional ETFs. They are designed for short-term tactical trading and not for long-term investment.

Investors should carefully consider a fund’s investment objectives, risks, charges, and expenses before investing. The prospectus or summary prospectus contains this and other important information about the fund and should be read carefully before investing. A prospectus or summary prospectus can be obtained here.

Investing involves risk. Principal loss is possible.

Distributor: Foreside Fund Services, LLC.

title: Australian Tech Earnings May Temper AI Disruption Concerns
content:
Bloomberg

(Bloomberg) -- Australia’s often overlooked technology sector may offer signs of resilience amid the global unease over artificial intelligence disruption, with a run of earnings reports showing steady gains.

Earnings season delivered growth among the seven companies in a local tech sub-index that reported first-half results in February, Bloomberg data show. Analysts also see the sector offering the strongest upside in the Australian market, with Bloomberg-compiled estimates implying a potential return of just under 80% over the next year.

Most Read from Bloomberg

LA’s Bankrupt ‘Graffiti Towers’ Finds Buyer for $470 Million An Insurance Expert Appraises the Safety Record of Self-Driving Cars Oslo Rebuilds Its Government Quarter with a New Focus: Openness New Tax Proposal Takes Aim at Thailand’s Salty Food Obsession

The solid results may help bolster the case that Australia’s technology companies can navigate global AI-driven volatility while continuing to deliver earnings growth. With valuations having eased from recent highs, focus will now shift to upcoming reports for clues on whether the sector can sustain its momentum.

“The recent pullback in technology stocks is not evidence of structural weakness but a recalibration,” said Emanuel Datt, principal at Datt Capital. The Australian tech sector has been punished by AI-related fears, “but they all seem to be reporting quite well,” he added.

Still, software firms across the board have been swept up by the so-called “AI scare trade” in recent weeks, as a string of updates from US startups including Anthropic PBC rattled sentiment toward the sector. Earlier this week, a report from little-known Citrini Research sparked a fresh selloff in delivery, payments and software companies.

Unlike markets in Taiwan, South Korea and China — which are home to some of the world’s largest AI hardware and semiconductor companies — Australia lacks comparable scale in AI infrastructure. Instead, its tech sector is concentrated in software and platform businesses that investors view as highly differentiated, commanding premium valuations relative to global peers, which some analysts see as under threat.

These companies have historically benefited from “scarcity value” in the local market, which has helped drive valuations high above global peers, said Armina Rosenberg, portfolio manager and co-founder at Minotaur Capital Management. “Now they’re going through an existential moment, and we’re seeing their premiums getting hit the hardest.”

Several technology names including logistics software maker WiseTech Global Ltd., trading platform IRESS Ltd. and booking platform SiteMinder Ltd. saw their shares rebound after posting results that topped expectations. WiseTech’s organic sales rose 7% for the six months ended Dec. 31, and in an effort to embrace efficiency generated from AI, the company said it will slash 30% of its workforce.

Story Continues

Many analysts argue the sector’s fundamentals remain intact. Morningstar Inc. strategists said earlier this month that Xero Ltd. and WiseTech may be relatively insulated from AI-related disruption because of their deep integration with customers’ core operations, while Citigroup Inc. analysts said Australian wealth platforms and portals faced comparatively lower exposure to AI-driven risks.

“The bigger question for Australian tech is whether these businesses can demonstrate that AI makes them more valuable rather than less, and the early earnings results are starting to provide some answers,” said Josh Gilbert, a market analyst at eToro.

Most Read from Bloomberg Businessweek

How Covid Quietly Rewires the Brain Indian Desserts Are Ready for Their Matcha Moment America’s Love of Ube Is Straining Supplies in the Philippines Americans Can’t Quit Steak, No Matter the Cost Supreme Court’s Tariff Ruling Is Secretly a Gift to Trump

©2026 Bloomberg L.P.

title: Old Mission joins Iress RSP network as market maker to enhance ETF execution for retail brokers
content:
London, Nov.  18, 2025  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Iress today announced that Old Mission, a global cross-asset market maker pricing exchange-traded funds (ETFs), has joined the Iress UK Retail Service Provider (RSP) network. The addition of Old Mission expands ETF coverage – one of the fastest-growing areas of retail trading – and enhances liquidity, pricing efficiency and execution quality for UK retail brokers and investors.

Old Mission’s participation brings deeper liquidity to the RSP network, enabling Iress retail customers to access a broader range of competitive ETF quotes and improved execution through a single connection. This collaboration strengthens Iress’s vision for a connected, transparent retail trading ecosystem that supports greater access, choice and confidence for investors.

Iress’ Executive General Manager, UK Trading, Debbie Kaye, said: “We’re delighted to welcome Old Mission as a market maker on the RSP network. Its expertise in ETFs and commitment to efficient, transparent market making strengthens the quality and diversity of liquidity available to Iress customers and supports our continued goal of delivering seamless market access and trade execution across asset classes.”

Lee Williams, Director of Old Mission Europe, said: “Joining the Iress RSP network is a natural extension of our strategy to provide liquidity to a wider range of counterparties. Iress’ established retail connectivity and breadth of coverage offers an ideal platform to reach retail brokers and platforms efficiently, ensuring that we maintain our focus on price quality and service to end investors.”

Old Mission went live for Iress customers on the RSP network at the end of October, with fast and frictionless onboarding and certification.

Ends

The Realization Group
Melanie Budden | Tina Kane
Email: melanie.budden@therealizationgroup.com | tina.kane@therealizationgroup.com

About Iress

Iress (IRE.ASX) is a technology company providing software to the financial services industry. We provide software and services for trading & market data, financial advice, investment management, life & pensions and data intelligence in Asia-Pacific, North America, Africa, the UK and Europe.

www.iress.com

title: Assessing Iren (BIT:IRE)’s Valuation After Recent Share Price Movements
content:
Iren (BIT:IRE) has caught the attention of investors after its recent price movements, even though there was no standout event driving the action. Sometimes, these quieter moments prompt the most interesting questions, especially for those weighing their next steps. Without a headline-grabbing announcement, the focus naturally returns to whether the current valuation reflects the company’s true prospects.

Across the year, Iren has delivered a total return of 33% and is up by nearly a third since the start of the year. This performance builds on a three-year return of 126%, showing considerable long-term strength. Shorter-term numbers, however, indicate some cooling, with only a slight gain over the past month and a small dip when looking at the past three months. This contrast hints at a period of consolidation, possibly a pause before renewed momentum or a sign the market is reassessing recent gains.

After these mixed movements, is the market giving Iren a critical breather before another run up, or is it already factoring in all available growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 2% Undervalued

The prevailing narrative views Iren as slightly undervalued, based on detailed forecasts and sector drivers rather than recent price action alone.

Ongoing and increased investments in water cycle management, electricity grid modernization, and sustainable waste infrastructure, including significant capital expenditures in the first half of the year for network upgrades and plant efficiencies, position Iren to benefit from Europe's push for resilient, upgraded infrastructure. This supports improved long-term operating margins and revenue growth.

This is not your average sector outlook. There is a bold financial thesis underlying this valuation, built on long-term margin expansion and infrastructure investments. Wondering what underlying profit, revenue, and future multiple assumptions drive this favorable view? The blueprint behind this perceived undervaluation is waiting to be uncovered by those ready to dig deeper.

Result: Fair Value of €2.64 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, rising competition and regulatory uncertainty could challenge Iren’s margin improvement, potentially changing the outlook if these headwinds persist or intensify.

Find out about the key risks to this Iren narrative.

Another View: What Does the DCF Model Say?

While the dominant market view suggests Iren is trading below its long-term potential, our DCF model offers a more conservative perspective and suggests the shares might actually be overvalued. Does the SWS DCF provide a reality check or miss the mark?

Story Continues

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.IRE Discounted Cash Flow as at Sep 2025

If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Build Your Own Iren Narrative

If you see things differently or want to dig into the numbers yourself, it takes just a few minutes to generate your own story. Do it your way.

title: Australia stocks higher at close of trade; S&P/ASX 200 up 0.43%
content:
Investing.com – Australia stocks were higher after the close on Monday, as gains in the Metals & Mining, Materials and Resources sectors led shares higher.

At the close in Sydney, the S&P/ASX 200 rose 0.43% to hit a new all time high.

The best performers of the session on the S&P/ASX 200 were  Pilbara Minerals Ltd  (ASX:PLS), which rose 19.69% or 0.38 points to trade at 2.31 at the close. Meanwhile, Mineral Resources Ltd (ASX:MIN) added 12.18% or 4.14 points to end at 38.12 and St Barbara Ltd (ASX:SBM) was up 9.38% or 0.03 points to 0.35 in late trade.

The worst performers of the session were JB Hi-Fi Ltd (ASX:JBH), which fell 8.39% or 9.87 points to trade at 107.83 at the close. AMP Ltd (ASX:AMP) declined 7.20% or 0.14 points to end at 1.74 and Iress Ltd (ASX:IRE) was down 7.23% or 0.68 points to 8.72.

Rising stocks outnumbered declining ones on the Sydney Stock Exchange by 612 to 553 and 454 ended unchanged.

The S&P/ASX 200 VIX, which measures the implied volatility of S&P/ASX 200 options, was up 2.56% to 10.89.

Gold Futures for December delivery was down 1.83% or 63.78 to $3,427.52 a troy ounce. Elsewhere in commodities trading, Crude oil for delivery in September fell 1.03% or 0.66 to hit $63.22 a barrel, while the October Brent oil contract fell 0.89% or 0.59 to trade at $66.00 a barrel.

AUD/USD was unchanged 0.00% to 0.65, while AUD/JPY fell 0.12% to 96.29.

The US Dollar Index Futures was down 0.08% at 97.93.

title: Australia stocks lower at close of trade; S&P/ASX 200 down 0.28%
content:
Investing.com – Australia stocks were lower after the close on Friday, as losses in the Healthcare, Financials and Consumer Staples sectors led shares lower.

At the close in Sydney, the S&P/ASX 200 lost 0.28%.

The best performers of the session on the S&P/ASX 200 were Iress Ltd (ASX:IRE), which rose 12.29% or 1.03 points to trade at 9.41 at the close. Meanwhile,  Pilbara Minerals Ltd  (ASX:PLS) added 9.18% or 0.16 points to end at 1.93 and AMP Ltd (ASX:AMP) was up 7.43% or 0.13 points to 1.88 in late trade.

The worst performers of the session were Qbe Insurance Group Ltd (ASX:QBE), which fell 8.83% or 2.07 points to trade at 21.38 at the close. Platinum Asset Management Ltd (ASX:PTM) declined 3.95% or 0.03 points to end at 0.73 and  Idp Education Ltd  (ASX:IEL) was down 3.65% or 0.16 points to 4.22.

Rising stocks outnumbered declining ones on the Sydney Stock Exchange by 558 to 535 and 431 ended unchanged.

Shares in AMP Ltd (ASX:AMP) rose to 5-year highs; up 7.43% or 0.13 to 1.88.

The S&P/ASX 200 VIX, which measures the implied volatility of S&P/ASX 200 options, was down 0.66% to 10.64.

Gold Futures for December delivery was up 1.34% or 46.17 to $3,499.87 a troy ounce. Elsewhere in commodities trading, Crude oil for delivery in September fell 0.88% or 0.56 to hit $63.32 a barrel, while the October Brent oil contract fell 0.77% or 0.51 to trade at $65.92 a barrel.

AUD/USD was unchanged 0.15% to 0.65, while AUD/JPY rose 0.13% to 96.11.

The US Dollar Index Futures was down 0.18% at 98.06.
